api/client/python/examples/async.purpose: Runnable async Python example scripts demonstrating openmeter SDK usage patterns against a live endpoint; each file is self-contained and exercises one domain (customers, entitlements, ingest, meter queries, subscriptions) using the async client from openmeter.aio.

patterns.name: Async context manager client lifetime
patterns.description: Always instantiate the async Client inside `async with Client(endpoint=..., token=...) as client:` so the underlying HTTP session is properly closed even on exceptions.
patterns.example: async with Client(endpoint=ENDPOINT, token=token) as client: ...

patterns.name: Environment-variable configuration with defaults
patterns.description: All connection and domain parameters read from os.environ with a fallback literal; never hardcode credentials.

patterns.name: HttpResponseError catch-all
patterns.description: Every main() wraps all client calls in a single try/except HttpResponseError block imported from corehttp.exceptions.

patterns.name: asyncio.run entry point
patterns.description: Scripts call asyncio.run(main()) at module level with no if __name__ == '__main__' guard.

patterns.name: Dict-access for discriminated-union list items
patterns.description: Entitlement list items from customer_entitlements_v2.list() come back as plain dicts due to SDK deserialisation; always use .get() not attribute access.

key_file_guides.role: Shows CloudEvents ingestion: construct Event with uuid id, UTC time, specversion='1.0', then await client.events.ingest_event(event).
key_file_guides.watch_for: time must be timezone-aware (datetime.timezone.utc); omitting tzinfo silently produces a naive datetime.

key_file_guides.role: Demonstrates three entitlement sub-types (metered/static/boolean) via get_customer_entitlement_value and customer_entitlements_v2.list.
key_file_guides.watch_for: items_property list returns dicts, not typed objects — attribute access raises AttributeError.

key_file_guides.role: Meter query examples: plain total, group_by list, and advanced_meter_group_by_filters with FilterString(eq=...).
key_file_guides.watch_for: r.data may be empty; always guard len(r.data) > 0 before indexing r.data[0].

key_file_guides.role: Full subscription lifecycle: create via PlanSubscriptionCreate, get_expanded, list_customer_subscriptions with status filter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: list_customer_subscriptions result pagination uses items_property not items.

key_file_guides.role: Customer CRUD: create with CustomerCreate, get by ID, update with CustomerReplaceUpdate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: client is instantiated inside the async with block, not at module level — unlike the sync counterpart.

anti_patterns: Using `from openmeter import Client` (sync) instead of `from openmeter.aio import Client` in async examples
Calling asyncio.run() inside an already-running event loop
Accessing entitlement list items with attribute syntax instead of .get() when the SDK returns dicts
Hardcoding endpoint or token instead of reading from environment variables
Instantiating the async Client outside `async with` — HTTP session is never closed

decisions.decision: One file per domain, not a single monolithic demo
decisions.rationale: Keeps each example runnable in isolation; developers can copy a single file without unrelated dependencies.

decisions.decision: async with for client lifetime instead of explicit close()
decisions.rationale: Guarantees HTTP session teardown even when exceptions are raised inside main().

code_examples.code:
import asyncio, datetime, uuid
from openmeter.aio import Client
from openmeter.models import Event
from corehttp.exceptions import HttpResponseError

ENDPOINT: str = environ.get('OPENMETER_ENDPOINT') or 'https://openmeter.cloud'
token = environ.get('OPENMETER_TOKEN')

async def main() -> None:
    async with Client(endpoint=ENDPOINT, token=token) as client:
        try:
            event = Event(
                id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
                source='my-app',
                specversion='1.0',
                type='prompt',
                subject='customer-1',
                time=datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc),
                data={'tokens': 100, 'model': 'gpt-4o'},
            )
            await client.events.ingest_event(event)
        except HttpResponseError as e:
            print(f'Error: {e}')

asyncio.run(main())

api/client/python/examples/sync.purpose: Runnable synchronous Python example scripts mirroring the async/ folder; each script exercises one domain using the blocking `openmeter.Client` and is structurally identical to its async counterpart except for module-level client construction and absence of async/await.

patterns.name: Module-level client instantiation
patterns.description: The sync Client is instantiated at module level (outside any function), not inside a context manager, because there is no async session to manage.

patterns.name: Environment-variable configuration with defaults
patterns.description: All connection and domain parameters read from os.environ with a fallback literal; never hardcode credentials.

patterns.name: HttpResponseError catch-all
patterns.description: Every main() wraps all client calls in a single try/except HttpResponseError block imported from corehttp.exceptions.

patterns.name: Direct main() call at module level
patterns.description: Scripts call main() at module level with no asyncio.run() and no if __name__ == '__main__' guard.

patterns.name: Dict-access for discriminated-union list items
patterns.description: Entitlement list items from customer_entitlements_v2.list() come back as plain dicts; always use .get() not attribute access.

key_file_guides.role: Blocking CloudEvents ingestion: module-level client, then client.events.ingest_event(event) with timezone-aware datetime.
key_file_guides.watch_for: time must be timezone-aware (datetime.timezone.utc); client is module-level, not a context manager.

key_file_guides.role: Same three-type entitlement demo as async counterpart using client.customer_entitlements_v2.list and client.customer_entitlement.get_customer_entitlement_value.
key_file_guides.watch_for: items_property returns dicts; attribute access on list items raises AttributeError.

key_file_guides.role: Meter queries including FilterString(eq=...) for advanced_meter_group_by_filters.
key_file_guides.watch_for: r.data may be empty; guard len(r.data) > 0 before indexing r.data[0].

key_file_guides.role: Subscription lifecycle: create with PlanSubscriptionCreate, get_expanded, list with SubscriptionStatus filter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: list result pagination uses items_property not items.

key_file_guides.role: Customer CRUD: create with CustomerCreate, get by ID, update with CustomerReplaceUpdate — all blocking calls on module-level client.
key_file_guides.watch_for: client is module-level here (unlike async/ which puts it inside async with); do not wrap in async with.

anti_patterns: Using `from openmeter.aio import Client` (async) instead of `from openmeter import Client` in sync examples
Wrapping the sync client in `async with` or calling asyncio.run()
Accessing entitlement list items with attribute syntax instead of .get() when the SDK returns dicts
Hardcoding endpoint or token instead of reading from environment variables
Instantiating the client inside main() on every call instead of once at module level

decisions.decision: Module-level client construction instead of per-call instantiation
decisions.rationale: Connection setup is done once; repeated calls reuse the same underlying HTTP connection pool.
Developers can compare sync and async examples side-by-side; the only difference is the import path (openmeter vs openmeter.aio) and absence of await/asyncio.run.

code_examples.code:
import datetime, uuid
from os import environ
from openmeter import Client
from openmeter.models import Event
from corehttp.exceptions import HttpResponseError

ENDPOINT: str = environ.get('OPENMETER_ENDPOINT') or 'https://openmeter.cloud'
token = environ.get('OPENMETER_TOKEN')

client = Client(endpoint=ENDPOINT, token=token)

def main() -> None:
    try:
        event = Event(
            id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            source='my-app',
            specversion='1.0',
            type='prompt',
            subject='customer-1',
            time=datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc),
            data={'tokens': 100, 'model': 'gpt-4o'},
        )
        client.events.ingest_event(event)
        print('Event ingested successfully')
    except HttpResponseError as e:
        print(f'Error ingesting event: {e}')

main()
